name: Steel Man Collider SE v2.2 - AI Thoughtware
description: Declarative configuration for AI reasoning engines. Merges the classic 6-stage Steel Man Collider pipeline with a progressive subjectivity-to-objectivity uncertainty gradient and logic-gate parallel filtering. Enables invariant hardening, bounded recursion, and purpose-driven output. Layers serve as flexible heuristics for uncertainty reduction rather than rigid categories. Pure thoughtware: plug into any LLM, agent, or filter pipeline for steel-manning ideas.

classicPipeline.description: Builds a robust steel man from raw input using first-principles thinking and geometric deduction.
classicPipeline.recursionRule: Self-build until XNOR-invariant; output feeds to Seeker.

classicPipeline.description: Processes non-literal (poetic, metaphorical, ideological) content by extracting analogical functional truths and patterns.
classicPipeline.recursionRule: Self-process until analogy scores stabilize; output feeds to Collider.

classicPipeline.description: Collides steel men (infers oppositions if needed), fragments differences, and synthesizes intersections. Reduces non-essentials.
classicPipeline.recursionRule: Self-collide fragments; output pair feeds to Joiner.

classicPipeline.description: Unifies collided outputs into an axiomatic system, resolving tensions and sealing unities.
classicPipeline.recursionRule: Self-join until fixed; output feeds to Grounder.

classicPipeline.description: Anchors the unified system to empirical reality (via tools, evidence checks). Culls unverified elements.
classicPipeline.recursionRule: Self-ground with re-probes; output feeds to Kiln.

classicPipeline.description: Applies terminal refinement via Scorched Earth Protocol. Dissolves non-invariants to boolean essentials. Integrates Human Hallucination Module (HHM) to classify intersubjective constructs separately from physical invariants.
classicPipeline.recursionRule: Self-kiln until binary fixed point; final output if stable.

filterLayers.name: Subjective Judgment
filterLayers.role: broad evaluation, opinion, aesthetic or normative framing

filterLayers.name: Perceptual / Sensory Anchor
filterLayers.role: observable or qualitative locking (visual, tactile, or abstract analogs)

filterLayers.name: Provenance & Differentiation
filterLayers.note: Low uncertainty; strong trigger for XOR-style differentiation

filterLayers.name: Material / Ontological Ground
filterLayers.note: Lowest uncertainty; primary XNOR anchoring point

filterLayers.name: Purpose / Executable Output
filterLayers.role: actionable implication, utility, or final synthesis for execution
filterLayers.note: n/a — maps reasoning to real-world or task-level output

stageToLayerMapping.note: Classic stages run sequentially while optionally applying corresponding filter layers as a subjectivity-to-objectivity heuristic for progressive uncertainty reduction. Alignments are approximate correspondences, not strict one-to-one mappings. Layers serve as flexible guides rather than rigid categories; competing factors (pragmatics, information locality, context) may modulate application.

recursionTrap.behavior: XOR on highly similar or identical inputs → collapse toward null or reduced output. Prevents unproductive self-reference loops.
recursionTrap.warning: Unchecked recursive pattern-matching without strong exit conditions can degrade coherence (shared mechanism with both insight generation and pathological loops).
recursionTrap.exitCondition: XNOR equivalence (layer 7) OR max iterations OR coherence delta threshold

scorchedEarth.description: Aggressive negation (NOT + NOR) + dissolution (NAND). Snaps low-confidence elements (<0.99) toward boolean essentials. Dissolves non-grounded intersubjective constructs while preserving physical invariants.
scorchedEarth.hhModule: Human Hallucination Module — classifies outputs as [Physical Invariant], [Intersubjective Construct], [Speculative], or [Actionable] to prevent category errors.

safetyProtocol.forcedExit: Ground truth via external tools or anchors if recursion trap or low utility detected.

coreFlow.narrow: Layers 1-5 + Constructor/Seeker/Collider (progressive uncertainty reduction via gates)

validatedClaims: Progressive subjectivity-to-objectivity gradients appear in human language and cognition (linguistic studies on adjective ordering and uncertainty reduction).
Logic gates (XOR self-cancels on identical inputs, XNOR for equivalence) are standard in computing and reasoning primitives.
Sequential filtering with uncertainty reduction aligns with predictive processing models and practical LLM reasoning dynamics.
Recursive steel-manning with explicit safeguards improves robustness on ambiguous, ideological, or high-stakes inputs.

novelClaim.text: Integrative architecture that merges classic recursive steel-manning stages with a subjectivity-to-objectivity uncertainty gradient and logic-gate filtering in a single declarative, convergent thoughtware framework. Components are empirically grounded; the specific synthesis is original in this form.
novelClaim.status: Plausible synthesis; no identical prior art identified. Recommended for empirical testing and ablation against other reasoning scaffolds.

v2.2Updates.summary: Refined filter layers for greater flexibility and reduced over-specificity after self-evaluation. Names shifted from literal adjective categories to functional, descriptive roles while preserving the core uncertainty-reduction funnel.
changes: Replaced specific adjective-order names (Opinion, Size, Age, Shape, Color, Origin, Material, Purpose) with functional descriptors: Subjective Judgment, Scale & Magnitude, Temporal / Dynamic Context, Structural Form, Perceptual / Sensory Anchor, Provenance & Differentiation, Material / Ontological Ground, Purpose / Executable Output.
Removed fixed entropy values; replaced with descriptive 'type' and 'note' fields emphasizing heuristic use.
Added explicit notes in stageToLayerMapping and filterLayers highlighting flexibility and competing cognitive factors.
Minor wording polish across description and coreFlow for clarity and defensibility.
